seoTitle: How to Change Auto Reply in Zendesk Triggers
metaDescription: Learn how to change the auto reply in Zendesk by duplicating the default trigger, editing the message text, and safely switching to your new version.
intro: Outdated confirmation text in your automatic reply can broadcast wrong business hours or a stale tone to every new customer. This guide shows how to safely update Zendesk's automated email confirmation by cloning the default trigger, editing the template, and activating the new version without breaking the original.

steps.heading: Open the Triggers panel
steps.body: In Admin Center, expand Objects and Rules and select Triggers to see your active business rules.

steps.heading: Find the default confirmation trigger
steps.body: Search for the default rule typically named something like Notify Requester of Received Request.

steps.heading: Clone it for safety
steps.body: Use the options menu to duplicate the standard trigger before making any edits, preserving a backup version.

steps.heading: Edit the message text
steps.body: Scroll to Actions and update the email subject line and notification body text in your cloned trigger.

steps.heading: Keep dynamic placeholders intact
steps.body: Preserve tokens like the ticket ID placeholder so the message still pulls in correct data for each customer.

steps.heading: Deactivate the old version and test
steps.body: Save your new trigger, deactivate the original, activate your new one, and submit a blank test ticket to confirm delivery.

faq.question: Why should I clone the trigger instead of editing it directly?
faq.answer: Cloning preserves a working backup in case something goes wrong while you edit the new version.

faq.question: What happens if I remove the ticket ID placeholder?
faq.answer: The email will no longer reference the correct ticket details dynamically, so it's best to keep core placeholders intact.

faq.question: How do I confirm the new auto-reply is actually going out?
faq.answer: Submit a blank public test case and check the outbound delivery in the ticket's event log.

faq.question: Can I have different auto-replies for different brands?
faq.answer: Yes, you can scope trigger conditions to specific brands or channels to send different confirmation text.
